preamble: The transformation from a voiced aspirated bilabial plosive ([bʱ]) to a voiceless aspirated bilabial plosive ([pʰ]) is a documented phonetic shift, primarily characterized by the devoicing of the stop while retaining its aspiration. This change is particularly observed in Indo-Aryan languages and their descendants.
phoneticEffects: Devoicing (loss of vocal fold vibration), Aspiration retention (the glottal breathiness following the release of the stop is preserved)

examples.note: Old Indo-Aryan 'to say' to Romani 'to say'

sources: Source via Google Search grounding (verify before merge)
